intro: Bradburya pubescens, commonly known as Bradburya, is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Bradburya pubescens include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Bradburya is often prepared in the form of infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
